source: If the lab is open, I make a small checklist for tomorrow step by step, since the deadline quietly shifted.
During lunch, I organize my locker for tomorrow just to be safe, so the weekend is less stressful.
Right after school, I finish the missing homework section just in case, while my phone stays on silent.
Before our next meet, I ask my sister to quiz me step by step, so the weekend is less stressful.
When the hallway clears out, I fix the code example in our report step by step, because practice runs late.
Before homeroom, I ask my sister to quiz me as a quick reset, without skipping dinner.
On the way to practice, I organize my locker for tomorrow step by step, so the weekend is less stressful.
If we finish early, I make a small checklist for tomorrow without making a big deal of it, and it keeps my week manageable.
When my teacher emails back, I clean up the shared doc without making a big deal of it, before everyone floods the chat.
Before our next meet, I review my notes for ten minutes just in case, since the deadline quietly shifted.
If we finish early, I clean up the shared doc just to be safe, so I do not lose track.
Before our next meet, I copy the new formulas into my notebook just to be safe, while the hallway is still calm.
Before homeroom, I fix the broken citation step by step, and I can actually sleep on time.
